intro: Eustachys uliginosa, also known as marsh-woolly bush, is a small shrub native to the southeastern United States. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and fever, due to its reported antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Eustachys uliginosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Eustachys uliginosa are teas and infusions, as well as tinctures and extracts, which are used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
